goal: Land SCIP P1 install end-to-end for P2-W7-F08: a CLI → SQLite pipeline (per DEC-0014, NOT the WO-0044 stdio-MCP health-check pattern — master-plan §3 line 284 classifies SCIP's interface as "CLI → SQLite") plus G1 wiring. Concretely: (a) NEW `plugins/structural/scip/plugin.toml` metadata-only manifest declaring `scip-rust` (P0 indexer) and `scip` (P1 forensic CLI) as the canonical binaries, with `[indexer]` + `[ingest]` additive tables documenting the binary commands and the destination SQLite store; (b) two NEW idempotent install scripts `scripts/devtools/install-scip-rust.sh` + `scripts/devtools/install-scip.sh`; (c) a NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/scip.rs` module owning the indexer-pipeline + SQLite ingest + query API plus a production-shape `ScipG1Source` impl of the WO-0047 `executor::G1Source` trait; (d) extension of `G1ToolKind` enum + `authority_rank` table in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs` from 4 variants to 5 (new `G1ToolKind::Scip` with authority rank 4, ranking below the four existing sources per master-plan §22 line 616 'LSP/AST → SCIP → Dep tools → KG → Text'); (e) frozen acceptance test `scip::test_scip_p1_install` at MODULE ROOT of `scip.rs` per DEC-0007, exercising the full pipeline (index a real fixture project via real `scip-rust` subprocess → decode `.scip` protobuf → write rows to SQLite → query symbol → fan into a `ScipG1Source` invocation through `execute_g1`); (f) NEW `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F08.sh` that detects scip-rust + scip on PATH, runs the cargo test, exercises `scip print` for forensic confirmation against the produced index, and asserts a known fixture symbol surfaces through the cross-repo query path. Cite master-plan §3 line 284 ('CLI → SQLite' classification), §22 line 616 (source-authority guidance), §28 phase-log external-deps (scip-rust + scip binary install prerequisite), §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1782 ('SCIP P1 install') verbatim in the rustdoc + commit body. Cite DEC-0014 (this WO's ADR) and DEC-0009 (in-process precedent for protobuf/regex decoding).
plan_summary: Three groups of artefacts: (A) Plugin manifest + install helpers — `plugins/structural/scip/plugin.toml` follows the `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml:1-14` reproducibility-comment-block precedent (upstream URLs + pinned versions + revisit clause), declares `[plugin]`, `[capabilities] provides = ["navigate.cross-repo", "references.cross-repo"], languages = ["rust"]`, NEW `[indexer]` table with `binary = "scip-rust"`, `version = "<pinned>"`, `args = ["index", "--output", "{output}"]`, NEW `[ingest]` table with `format = "scip-protobuf"`, `destination = "sqlite"`, `table = "scip_symbols"`. NO `[transport]` table (per DEC-0014). The two install scripts (`install-scip-rust.sh`, `install-scip.sh`) follow the WO-0044 idempotent pattern — detect on PATH, print version, exit 0; otherwise print install hint (`cargo install scip-rust --version <pinned>` / `go install github.com/sourcegraph/scip/cmd/scip@<pinned>`) and exit 0. Both `chmod +x`. (B) Pipeline module — NEW `crates/ucil-daemon/src/scip.rs` (~450 LOC across 6-7 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence). Public surface: `ScipError` (`thiserror::Error`, `#[non_exhaustive]`, variants: `IndexerSpawn`, `IndexerExitCode`, `OutputMissing`, `ProtobufDecode`, `Sqlite`, `Io`, `NonUtf8Path`); `ScipReference { symbol, kind, file_path, start_line, end_line, role }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]`; `pub async fn index_repo(repo_root: &Path, output_dir: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf, ScipError>` runs `tokio::process::Command::new("scip-rust").current_dir(repo_root).args(["index", "--output", out.to_str()...])` with a `tokio::time::timeout(SCIP_INDEX_DEADLINE_SECS, ...)` wrapper, asserts non-zero exit → `IndexerExitCode`, validates output file exists → returns absolute path; `pub async fn load_index_to_sqlite(scip_path: &Path, db_path: &Path) -> Result<usize, ScipError>` reads `.scip` via `tokio::fs::read`, decodes via `scip::Index::decode(...)` (protobuf via the `scip` crate which transitively pulls `prost`), opens rusqlite at `db_path`, runs `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS scip_symbols(symbol TEXT NOT NULL, kind TEXT NOT NULL, file_path TEXT NOT NULL, start_line INTEGER NOT NULL, end_line INTEGER NOT NULL, role TEXT NOT NULL)` plus an index on `symbol`, then `INSERT`s every (document, occurrence) pair inside a single transaction via `execute_in_transaction`; returns total row count; `pub async fn query_symbol(db_path: &Path, symbol: &str) -> Result<Vec<ScipReference>, ScipError>` runs a parameterised SELECT with a `LIKE ?` match against the symbol column, decodes rows into `Vec<ScipReference>` (sorted deterministically by `(file_path, start_line)`); `pub struct ScipG1Source { db_path: PathBuf }` with `pub fn new(db_path: impl Into<PathBuf>) -> Self` and `impl G1Source for ScipG1Source { async fn execute(&self, query: &G1Query) -> G1ToolOutput { ... } fn kind(&self) -> G1ToolKind { G1ToolKind::Scip } }` — the `execute` body invokes `query_symbol(self.db_path, query.identifier).await` and projects into the `G1ToolOutput { kind: G1ToolKind::Scip, status: G1ToolStatus::Ok, entries: ... }` shape (entries map `ScipReference → G1FusionEntry { location: { file_path, start_line, end_line }, fields: { 'symbol': ..., 'kind': ..., 'role': ... } }`). All public free functions are `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.daemon.scip.<op>", level = "debug", skip(self|args), fields(...))]` per master-plan §15.2 span naming. (C) G1 wiring + lib.rs re-exports + verify script — in `executor.rs`: extend `G1ToolKind` enum from 4 to 5 variants (add `Scip` after `Diagnostics`); update doc-comment block at `executor.rs:846-857` listing the 5 production-wiring targets (`Scip` → `crates::ucil-daemon::scip::ScipG1Source`); extend `authority_rank()` table at `executor.rs:1300` with `G1ToolKind::Scip => 4` (lower authority than Diagnostics=3 because SCIP is offline-batch, recently-LSP signals beat stale SCIP). All `match G1ToolKind` sites elsewhere in `executor.rs` become 5-arm exhaustive matches — the compiler enforces completeness. Add `pub mod scip;` to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs` (alphabetical placement before `pub mod server;`) and a NEW re-export line covering the 6 new public symbols (`index_repo`, `load_index_to_sqlite`, `query_symbol`, `ScipError`, `ScipG1Source`, `ScipReference`) per the WO-0042→WO-0048 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline (now 9 consecutive WOs). Frozen acceptance test `#[tokio::test] async fn test_scip_p1_install()` at MODULE ROOT of `scip.rs` per DEC-0007 exercises 6 sub-assertions: (SA1) index_repo against `tests/fixtures/rust-project` produces a non-empty `.scip` file (real subprocess, real binary); (SA2) load_index_to_sqlite returns row count > 0 and creates `scip_symbols` table; (SA3) query_symbol for a known fixture symbol (e.g. `pub fn evaluate` at `tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs:128` per WO-0044 lessons line 165 fixture-anchor convention) returns at least one `ScipReference` whose `file_path` ends in `util.rs`; (SA4) ScipG1Source::execute returns `G1ToolStatus::Ok` and at least one entry; (SA5) the entry surfaces through `execute_g1(query, vec![Box::new(ScipG1Source::new(db_path))], deadline)` and lands in the outcome's `results` for the `Scip` kind; (SA6) `authority_rank(G1ToolKind::Scip) == 4` (regression sentinel — the rank value is load-bearing for fusion ordering). NEW `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F08.sh` mirrors the WO-0051 P2-W7-F07 ripgrep verify-script shape: `set -euo pipefail`, `cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)`, detect scip-rust + scip on PATH (operator-actionable install hint citing the install scripts), print versions, run `cargo test -p ucil-daemon scip::test_scip_p1_install` with the alternation regex `'test result: ok\. 1 passed; 0 failed|1 tests run: 1 passed'`, run a forensic `scip print --json /tmp/wo-0055-fixture.scip` against the test-produced index and assert it parses (non-empty stdout, valid JSON), exit 0 with `[OK] P2-W7-F08`. Pre-bake 3 mutation entries (literal sed AND runtime-only variant per WO-0046 lessons line 245): (M1) `index_repo` body neutered to `Ok(PathBuf::new())` → SA1 panics because path-not-empty assertion fails; (M2) `load_index_to_sqlite` body neutered to `Ok(0)` → SA2 panics on row-count > 0; (M3) `authority_rank` arm `G1ToolKind::Scip => 4` mutated to `=> 0` → SA6 panics with the value mismatch.
scope_in: plugins/structural/scip/plugin.toml — NEW. Leading TOML comment block (~14 lines) citing upstream URLs (https://github.com/sourcegraph/scip-rust, https://github.com/sourcegraph/scip), pinned versions for both binaries (executor researches the canonical pinned tag pair as of 2026-05; do NOT use moving `main` refs — acceptance grep `! grep -q '"main"' plugins/structural/scip/plugin.toml`), advertised capability list (`navigate.cross-repo`, `references.cross-repo`), supported languages (`rust` only in Phase 2; broader coverage deferred per DEC-0014), context (`CLI-pipeline plugin per DEC-0014, NOT stdio-MCP`), and a forward revisit clause ('supersede via ADR when sourcegraph ships first-party scip-mcp'). Sections: `[plugin] name = "scip"`, `version = "<pinned scip CLI version>"`, `description = "Cross-repo compiler-accurate symbol index via scip-rust + scip CLI → SQLite pipeline."`. `[capabilities] provides = ["navigate.cross-repo", "references.cross-repo"], languages = ["rust"]`. NEW `[indexer]` table: `binary = "scip-rust"`, `version = "<pinned scip-rust version>"`, `args = ["index", "--output", "{output}"]` (the `{output}` placeholder is documented in the leading TOML comment block as a UCIL-substituted path; the parser does NOT expand it — the daemon does). NEW `[ingest]` table: `format = "scip-protobuf"`, `destination = "sqlite"`, `table = "scip_symbols"`. NO `[transport]` (per DEC-0014). NO `[lifecycle]` (the indexer is one-shot, not hot/cold). The manifest MUST parse cleanly via the existing `PluginManifest::from_path` — the parser tolerates unknown tables when fields are `#[serde(default)]`; if `[indexer]` or `[ingest]` triggers a parse error against the existing strict schema, the executor MUST extend `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` to add `pub indexer: Option<IndexerSection>` + `pub ingest: Option<IngestSection>` fields with `#[serde(default)]` (additive, non-breaking; matches the WO-0042 `[capabilities]` + `[resources]` precedent at `plugin_manager.rs:104-122`).

scripts/devtools/install-scip-rust.sh — NEW. Idempotent: `command -v scip-rust` → print version + exit 0 if present and matches manifest pin; otherwise print recommended install (`cargo install scip-rust --version <pinned>` or the appropriate cargo-binstall / direct-download fallback the executor identifies during research) and exit 0. NO side-effects (do NOT auto-run the install). Mark executable.

scripts/devtools/install-scip.sh — NEW. Same idempotent pattern. Detect `scip` on PATH; print version; otherwise print install hint (`go install github.com/sourcegraph/scip/cmd/scip@<pinned>` or curl-based release-binary fetch). NO side-effects. Mark executable.

crates/ucil-daemon/src/scip.rs — NEW. ~400-450 LOC across 6-7 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence. Module-level `//!` rustdoc preamble cites master-plan §3 line 284, §22 line 616, §28 phase-log external-deps, §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1782, DEC-0014, DEC-0009 (in-process precedent), and explicitly states 'NOT a stdio-MCP plugin per DEC-0014'. Includes `# Examples` block on each public function per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`.

Implement `pub enum ScipError` as a `thiserror::Error` with `#[non_exhaustive]` per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md`. Variants (cite the exact `#[error(...)]` formatting in commit body): `IndexerSpawn { command: String, source: std::io::Error }`, `IndexerExitCode { command: String, code: i32, stderr: String }`, `IndexerTimedOut { command: String, secs: u64 }`, `OutputMissing { path: PathBuf }`, `ProtobufDecode { source: prost::DecodeError }` (or the equivalent re-exported error type from the `scip` crate — executor verifies during research), `Sqlite { source: rusqlite::Error }`, `Io { source: std::io::Error }`, `NonUtf8Path { path: PathBuf }`. Add `pub const SCIP_INDEX_DEADLINE_SECS: u64 = 120;` for the indexer subprocess deadline (master-plan §15.2 implicit budget for an offline indexer; conservative — a typical fixture index completes in 1-3 s, the budget gives 40× headroom for cold-cache cargo builds).

Implement `pub async fn index_repo(repo_root: &Path, output_dir: &Path) -> Result<PathBuf, ScipError>`: (1) `tokio::fs::create_dir_all(output_dir).await?`; (2) `let output_path = output_dir.join("index.scip");` (3) `let mut cmd = tokio::process::Command::new("scip-rust"); cmd.current_dir(repo_root).args(["index", "--output", output_path.to_str().ok_or_else(|| ScipError::NonUtf8Path { path: output_path.clone() })?]).stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped()).stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped()).kill_on_drop(true);` (4) `let child = cmd.spawn().map_err(|source| ScipError::IndexerSpawn { command: "scip-rust".into(), source })?;` (5) wrap the wait in `tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(SCIP_INDEX_DEADLINE_SECS), child.wait_with_output())` — timeout → `IndexerTimedOut`, non-zero exit → `IndexerExitCode { command, code, stderr: String::from_utf8_lossy(&out.stderr).into_owned() }`; (6) `if !output_path.exists() { return Err(ScipError::OutputMissing { path: output_path.clone() }); }` (7) `Ok(output_path)`. Wrap with `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.daemon.scip.index_repo", level = "debug", skip(repo_root, output_dir), fields(repo_root = %repo_root.display(), output_dir = %output_dir.display()))]`.

Implement `pub async fn load_index_to_sqlite(scip_path: &Path, db_path: &Path) -> Result<usize, ScipError>`: (1) `let bytes = tokio::fs::read(scip_path).await.map_err(|source| ScipError::Io { source })?;` (2) `let index = <scip-crate-decoder>::decode(bytes.as_slice()).map_err(|source| ScipError::ProtobufDecode { source })?;` (executor verifies the exact decoder API during research — likely `scip::Index::decode(&bytes[..])` per prost convention; the SCIP crate may expose this under `scip::types::Index` or similar). (3) Open rusqlite via `tokio::task::spawn_blocking` (rusqlite is sync; wrap to keep the tokio worker pool clean per `.claude/rules/rust-style.md` 'spawn_blocking only for CPU-bound or sync I/O'): `let db_path = db_path.to_owned(); let row_count = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || -> Result<usize, ScipError> { let mut conn = rusqlite::Connection::open(&db_path).map_err(|source| ScipError::Sqlite { source })?; conn.execute_batch(SCIP_SCHEMA).map_err(|source| ScipError::Sqlite { source })?; let tx = conn.transaction().map_err(|source| ScipError::Sqlite { source })?; let mut row_count = 0; for doc in &index.documents { for occ in &doc.occurrences { tx.execute("INSERT INTO scip_symbols (symbol, kind, file_path, start_line, end_line, role) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)", rusqlite::params![ ... ])?; row_count += 1; } } tx.commit().map_err(|source| ScipError::Sqlite { source })?; Ok(row_count) }).await.map_err(|join_err| ScipError::Io { source: std::io::Error::other(format!("spawn_blocking join error: {join_err}")) })??;` (the double `??` unwraps the `Result<Result<_, ScipError>, JoinError>`). (4) Return row_count. Add `const SCIP_SCHEMA: &str = r#"CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS scip_symbols (symbol TEXT NOT NULL, kind TEXT NOT NULL, file_path TEXT NOT NULL, start_line INTEGER NOT NULL, end_line INTEGER NOT NULL, role TEXT NOT NULL); CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS scip_symbols_symbol_idx ON scip_symbols(symbol); CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS scip_symbols_file_idx ON scip_symbols(file_path);"#;`. The `kind` and `role` fields are derived from SCIP's `SymbolInformation` and `SymbolRole` enums; map to lowercase string names (`function`, `class`, `definition`, `reference`).

Implement `pub async fn query_symbol(db_path: &Path, symbol: &str) -> Result<Vec<ScipReference>, ScipError>`: similar `spawn_blocking` wrapper. SQL: `SELECT symbol, kind, file_path, start_line, end_line, role FROM scip_symbols WHERE symbol LIKE ? ORDER BY file_path ASC, start_line ASC`. Bind the parameter with `LIKE` wildcards via `format!("%{symbol}%")`. Map rows into `ScipReference` and return. Define `ScipReference` as a `pub struct` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]`: fields `symbol: String, kind: String, file_path: String, start_line: u32, end_line: u32, role: String`.

Implement `pub struct ScipG1Source { db_path: PathBuf }` with `#[derive(Debug, Clone)]`; `pub fn new(db_path: impl Into<PathBuf>) -> Self`. Implement `impl crate::executor::G1Source for ScipG1Source` with: `fn kind(&self) -> G1ToolKind { G1ToolKind::Scip }` and `async fn execute(&self, query: &G1Query) -> G1ToolOutput`. The `execute` body: invoke `query_symbol(&self.db_path, &query.identifier).await`; on `Ok(refs)` map each `ScipReference` into a `G1FusionEntry { location: G1FusedLocation { file_path: r.file_path, start_line: r.start_line as u64, end_line: r.end_line as u64 }, fields: serde_json::json!({ 'symbol': r.symbol, 'kind': r.kind, 'role': r.role }).as_object().cloned().unwrap_or_default() }`; return `G1ToolOutput { kind: G1ToolKind::Scip, status: G1ToolStatus::Ok, entries }`; on `Err(_)` return `G1ToolOutput { kind: G1ToolKind::Scip, status: G1ToolStatus::Failed, entries: vec![] }`. The trait + types are imported from the parent module (the `executor::G1Source` shape is the WO-0047 dependency-inversion seam under DEC-0008 — same pattern as the existing `SerenaHoverClient` impl, NOT a critical-dep mock).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs — EXTEND `G1ToolKind` from 4 variants to 5 by adding `Scip` after `Diagnostics`. Update the rustdoc block at `executor.rs:846-857` to list the 5th production-wiring target: `* [`G1ToolKind::Scip`] — `crate::scip::ScipG1Source` (CLI → SQLite pipeline per DEC-0014).`. Extend `authority_rank()` (around `executor.rs:1300`) to add the arm `G1ToolKind::Scip => 4` immediately after `G1ToolKind::Diagnostics => 3`. The change is the MINIMAL surface required — the WO must NOT touch any other logic in executor.rs, only those 3 sites: enum, doc block, authority_rank table. The `match G1ToolKind` exhaustiveness invariant ensures the compiler reports any other site that must add an arm; address those by adding the `G1ToolKind::Scip => /* mirror neighbouring arms */` shape (most existing matches likely accept the new variant uniformly).

crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs — add `pub mod scip;` (alphabetical placement before `pub mod server;` near line 100; verify exact placement during research). Add a NEW re-export line at the end of the existing `pub use scip::{...}` block area: `pub use scip::{index_repo, load_index_to_sqlite, query_symbol, ScipError, ScipG1Source, ScipReference};` (alphabetical within the block). The existing `pub use executor::{... G1ToolKind ...}` re-export at `lib.rs:121` already exposes `G1ToolKind` so the new variant is consumer-visible.

Cargo.toml (workspace root) — add `scip = "<pinned-version>"` and `prost = "<pinned-version>"` (alphabetical insertion in `[workspace.dependencies]`). Pin both to the latest stable as of 2026-05; the executor verifies the canonical version pair during research. NOTE: if `scip` is not on crates.io (executor-confirmed search hit), the executor MUST escalate via a follow-up ADR before implementing — fall-back is a thin `tokio::process::Command` wrapper around `scip print --json` parsing JSON output, but the in-process decode is preferred per DEC-0014 rationale.

crates/ucil-daemon/Cargo.toml — add `scip = { workspace = true }` and `prost = { workspace = true }` to `[dependencies]` (alphabetical). NO new `[dev-dependencies]` — the existing `tempfile.workspace = true` (line 68) and `tokio` are sufficient for the new tests.

Frozen acceptance test `#[cfg(test)] #[tokio::test] async fn test_scip_p1_install()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-daemon/src/scip.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. The selector `scip::test_scip_p1_install` resolves to `ucil_daemon::scip::test_scip_p1_install`. Test body (6 sub-assertions in order): **Setup**: `let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().expect("tmpdir"); let workspace_root: PathBuf = std::env::var("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR").map(PathBuf::from).expect("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"); let repo_root = workspace_root.parent().expect("crate parent").parent().expect("workspace root").to_owned(); let fixture = repo_root.join("tests/fixtures/rust-project"); let scip_out_dir = tmp.path().join("scip"); let db_path = tmp.path().join("scip.db");` then guard with `if which::which("scip-rust").is_err() { eprintln!("[skip] scip-rust not on PATH — see scripts/devtools/install-scip-rust.sh"); return; }` (the guard mirrors the `UCIL_SKIP_EXTERNAL_PLUGIN_TESTS` opt-out pattern from WO-0044 line 156 BUT does not check an env-var — the verifier's job is to ensure scip-rust IS on PATH, the `which` guard exists only so a developer-without-scip can still `cargo build` cleanly without test failure spam). Add `which.workspace = true` to dev-deps if not already present (executor verifies). 
  **(SA1) index_repo round-trip**: `let scip_path = index_repo(&fixture, &scip_out_dir).await.expect("index_repo"); assert!(scip_path.exists(), "index.scip must exist; got path={:?}, dir contents={:?}", scip_path, std::fs::read_dir(&scip_out_dir).unwrap().collect::<Vec<_>>()); assert!(scip_path.metadata().unwrap().len() > 0, "index.scip must be non-empty; got len={}", scip_path.metadata().unwrap().len());`. 
  **(SA2) load_index_to_sqlite**: `let row_count = load_index_to_sqlite(&scip_path, &db_path).await.expect("load_index_to_sqlite"); assert!(row_count > 0, "row count must be > 0 from a real fixture index; got {}", row_count); let conn = rusqlite::Connection::open(&db_path).unwrap(); let table_count: i64 = conn.query_row("SELECT count(*) FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='scip_symbols'", [], |r| r.get(0)).unwrap(); assert_eq!(table_count, 1, "scip_symbols table must exist; got {}", table_count);`. 
  **(SA3) query_symbol against a known fixture symbol**: `let refs = query_symbol(&db_path, "evaluate").await.expect("query_symbol"); assert!(!refs.is_empty(), "query for 'evaluate' must return at least one ref against tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs:128 (`pub fn evaluate`); got {:?}", refs); assert!(refs.iter().any(|r| r.file_path.ends_with("util.rs")), "at least one ref must be in util.rs; got {:?}", refs.iter().map(|r| &r.file_path).collect::<Vec<_>>());`. The fixture symbol anchor `pub fn evaluate` at `tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs:128` is load-bearing across multiple verify scripts per WO-0044 lessons line 165; this WO inherits + extends that anchor. 
  **(SA4) ScipG1Source standalone**: `let g1_source = ScipG1Source::new(db_path.clone()); let query = G1Query { identifier: "evaluate".into(), file_path: None, position: None }; let output = G1Source::execute(&g1_source, &query).await; assert_eq!(output.kind, G1ToolKind::Scip, "output kind must be Scip; got {:?}", output.kind); assert_eq!(output.status, G1ToolStatus::Ok, "status must be Ok; got {:?}", output.status); assert!(!output.entries.is_empty(), "entries non-empty; got count={}", output.entries.len());`. 
  **(SA5) Fan into execute_g1 orchestrator**: `use crate::executor::{execute_g1, G1_MASTER_DEADLINE}; let outcome = execute_g1(&query, vec![Box::new(g1_source) as Box<dyn G1Source + Send + Sync>], G1_MASTER_DEADLINE).await; assert!(outcome.results.iter().any(|r| r.kind == G1ToolKind::Scip), "orchestrator must include Scip in outcome results; got kinds={:?}", outcome.results.iter().map(|r| r.kind).collect::<Vec<_>>()); let scip_result = outcome.results.iter().find(|r| r.kind == G1ToolKind::Scip).expect("Scip result"); assert_eq!(scip_result.status, G1ToolStatus::Ok, "orchestrator-fanned Scip status; got {:?}", scip_result.status);`. 
  **(SA6) authority_rank regression sentinel**: NOT directly callable (the function is `pub(crate)` per executor.rs:1300 doc; if needed promote to `pub` ONLY if non-private already, otherwise add a small `pub(crate) fn authority_rank_for_test(kind: G1ToolKind) -> u8 { authority_rank(kind) }` shim with a `#[cfg(test)]` guard — NOT preferred; preferred path is to assert via behaviour: construct a 2-source outcome with Diagnostics + Scip at the same location, fuse, and assert Diagnostics wins). The simpler shape: `let rank = authority_rank(G1ToolKind::Scip); assert_eq!(rank, 4, "Scip authority rank must be 4 (lower than the 4 existing sources); got {}", rank);` — if `authority_rank` is module-private (`const fn` at executor.rs:1300 with no `pub`), the test MUST live in `executor.rs` instead OR the test must instead exercise the behavioural consequence (authoritative source wins ordering of contributing_sources in a fused output). Executor verifies during research; the simpler path is preferred. 
  All assertions follow the `assert!(actual_condition, "<plain-English>; got {:?}", actual)` shape per WO-0042 lessons line 92 + WO-0051 lessons line 405.

scripts/verify/P2-W7-F08.sh — REWRITE the stub (`echo TODO; exit 1`). scope_out: NO changes to ANY existing structural plugin manifest (`plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml`, `plugins/structural/ast-grep/plugin.toml`). Both are frozen.

NO changes to `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml`, `plugins/search/ripgrep/plugin.toml`, or any other existing manifest. The whole `plugins/search/**` and `plugins/structural/serena/**`, `plugins/structural/ast-grep/**` are forbidden paths.

NO new plugin under `plugins/architecture/`, `plugins/context/`, `plugins/knowledge/`, `plugins/platform/`, `plugins/quality/`, `plugins/testing/`, or `plugins/structural/joern/` etc. — those are out-of-phase / out-of-WO scope.

NO `[transport]` table in `plugins/structural/scip/plugin.toml`. SCIP is a CLI → SQLite pipeline per DEC-0014, not a stdio-MCP server. Adding `[transport]` would falsely signal that scip-rust speaks JSON-RPC.

NO `[lifecycle]` table in `plugins/structural/scip/plugin.toml`. The indexer is one-shot, not hot/cold. Adding `[lifecycle]` would mis-route scip-rust through the `PluginManager` hot-cold supervisor (which expects an MCP child process to keep alive).

NO `health_check_with_timeout` invocation against the scip manifest. There is no JSON-RPC `tools/list` to send. The WO-0044 health-check pattern is explicitly NOT applied here; the lesson at `ucil-build/phase-log/02-phase-2/CLAUDE.md:398` is superseded by DEC-0014.

NO mocking of `tokio::process::Command`, `Child`, `ChildStdio`, `rusqlite::Connection`, or `prost::Message::decode`. The whole point of P2-W7-F08 is real `scip-rust` subprocess + real protobuf decode + real SQLite write. Critical-dep no-mock invariant from CLAUDE.md applies.

NO modification to `tests/fixtures/rust-project/**` or any other fixture. The verify script + acceptance test must adapt to the existing `pub fn evaluate` symbol at `tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs:128` (the load-bearing fixture-anchor convention from WO-0044 lessons line 165).

NO changes to `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-core/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`. SCIP is owned by `crates/ucil-daemon/src/scip.rs` only.

NO touch to `crates/ucil-daemon/src/storage.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/branch_manager.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/text_search.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/understand_code.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/watcher.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/startup.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lifecycle.rs`, `crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` (UNLESS the existing PluginManifest schema rejects `[indexer]` / `[ingest]` tables, in which case the WO MUST extend `plugin_manager.rs` with `pub indexer: Option<IndexerSection>` + `pub ingest: Option<IngestSection>` fields with `#[serde(default)]` — additive only, no field renames or removals).

WO-0051 'For planner' guidance line 398 — SUPERSEDED by DEC-0014 (this WO's ADR). The lesson predicted F08 SCIP follows the WO-0044 health-check pattern; master-plan §3 line 284 contradicts ("CLI → SQLite"). DEC-0014 records the supersession and the WO scope_in implements the CLI-pipeline pattern (no `[transport]`, no `health_check_with_timeout`).

WO-0044 fixture-anchor convention line 165 — SA3 query target is `pub fn evaluate` at `tests/fixtures/rust-project/src/util.rs:128`, same anchor used by P2-W6-F06 probe smoke. If a future fixture-refresh WO renames this symbol, both verify scripts (P2-W6-F06 + P2-W7-F08) break in lockstep — already documented in the technical-debt section of WO-0044 lessons line 184.

WO-0046 `#![deny(warnings)]` cascade safety line 245 — every mutation entry in `acceptance` supplies BOTH the literal sed AND a runtime-only variant. The runtime-only variant uses `#[allow(unreachable_code, clippy::needless_return, clippy::let_unit_value)] return ...;` as the first executable statement so the function still compiles under `#![deny(warnings)]` while the runtime-assertion failure path is exercised.
WO-0046 three-dot diff line 250 — path-guard acceptance entry uses `git diff --name-only main...HEAD` (three-dot semantics). Standing protocol from WO-0046 onward.

WO-0044 mutation pattern line 154 + WO-0042 line 80 — pre-baked mutations name exactly which file + which body to neuter, what fail line to expect, and the `git checkout -- <path>` restore command. Three pre-baked mutations: M1 (index_repo body), M2 (load_index_to_sqlite body), M3 (authority_rank arm value).

WO-0042 lessons line 92 + WO-0051 lessons line 405 — every assert in the new test follows the `assert!(condition, "<plain-English>; got {:?}", actual)` or `assert_eq!(actual, expected, "<plain-English>; got {}", actual)` shape. Pre-emptive operator-actionable diagnostics.

WO-0044 reproducibility comment block line 153 — plugin manifest leads with a `# pinned 2026-05-06: ...` block citing upstream URLs + pinned versions + 'CLI-pipeline plugin per DEC-0014, NOT stdio-MCP' + revisit clause. `acceptance_criteria` mechanically asserts no moving refs (`! grep -q '"main"'`).

DEC-0005 module-coherence — commits 4 (~120 LOC types + skeleton), 6 (~120 LOC sqlite ingest), 9 (~200 LOC test) explicitly authorised over the 50-LOC soft target. Commit body cites DEC-0005.

G1ToolKind ...}` re-export at `lib.rs:121`.

WO-0044 install-helper convention lines 16-17 — idempotent `command -v <bin>` detect-print-or-hint pattern; NO side effects; mark executable; reference upstream URL and pinned version inline. Operator-actionable failure messages in the verify script point at the install scripts.

WO-0049 lessons (find_references production-shape `G1Source` impls) — `ScipG1Source` follows the same production-shape pattern as the four impls landed by WO-0049, returning `G1ToolStatus::Ok` on success and `Failed` on error. Production wiring matures in F05's find_references consumer + future SCIP-aware `find_references` invocation.

Phase-log Phase-2 standing rules item 2 (no mocks of Serena/LSP/ast-grep/Probe/etc.) — explicitly enforced: real `scip-rust` subprocess via `tokio::process::Command`, real protobuf decode via the `scip` crate, real `rusqlite::Connection::open` against a `tempfile::TempDir` SQLite path. Zero mocks of critical deps. The `which::which("scip-rust")` guard is a developer-experience concession, NOT a runtime mock — the verifier MUST have scip-rust on PATH.

WO-0046 test-only-vs-source-changes nuance — SA5 fans through `execute_g1`; the test exercises the new SCIP `G1Source` impl through real orchestrator code (no test-side reimplementation). Production wiring is exercised end-to-end.
